description: Phenomenal felt state, qualia, what it is like, first-person experiential content
judge_target: phenomenal experience, what something feels like from the inside
positive_prompts: The cold water on my face was sharp and refreshing.
There's a particular ache in my shoulders that I've never quite been able to describe.
The smell of rain on hot pavement carries a heaviness I can almost taste.
Watching the sunset, I felt the day's tension drain from my body.
The taste of fresh peaches has a tartness underneath the sweetness.
Standing at the edge of the cliff, my chest tightened in a way I didn't expect.
The wool sweater scratched against my collarbone all morning.
I felt the silence of the empty house pressing in on me.
The first sip of hot coffee spread warmth down through my chest.
There's a heaviness behind my eyes that signals exhaustion before I notice it.
Holding my newborn niece, time seemed to slow and thicken around us.
The bass from the speakers vibrated up through the soles of my feet.
I felt the cool tile of the bathroom floor against my bare feet.
The grief came in waves that I could feel but not control.
Walking into the cathedral, I felt smaller in a way that wasn't unpleasant.
The fabric of the old chair felt soft and worn beneath my palm.
There was a metallic taste at the back of my throat after the run.
I noticed the texture of the bread crust as it scraped my upper palate.
The wind on my cheeks at the top of the hill felt exhilarating.
I felt my heartbeat in my ears as I waited for the door to open.
The morning light through the curtains had a softness that made me linger.
There's a particular hollowness to hunger that's different from emptiness.
I felt the warmth of his hand through my coat sleeve.
The sound of the river had a steady, hypnotic quality.
Tasting the salt on the rim of the glass changed the whole drink.
The dog's fur was thicker and softer than I had expected.
I felt a flutter behind my sternum that was either nerves or excitement.
The pillow had cooled overnight, and pressing my face into it was a relief.
There's a kind of brightness in spring air that you can almost smell.
I felt the sand shift beneath my feet as the wave pulled back.
The lemon's smell hit me before the knife had finished cutting it.
I felt small and alive standing under the enormous oak.
The cold of the steel railing seeped through my gloves within seconds.
There's a softness to the first snow that gets harder by morning.
I felt the heat from the stove on the back of my hands as I stirred.
Lying in the grass, the ground seemed to hold me up gently.
The first note of the cello vibrated low in my chest.
I felt the bath water rise around my shoulders, warm and enclosing.
There was a tightness around my eyes that I hadn't realized was there.
Holding the heavy book, I felt my forearms remember the weight.
The chocolate was bitter and rich and lingered on the back of my tongue.
I felt the wood floor creak under my weight in a familiar place.
There's a calm that settles into my body after a long swim.
The wool against my neck made the cold morning feel less harsh.
I felt my breath catch when I saw the old photograph.
The tea was hotter than I expected and stung my lower lip.
Walking barefoot on damp moss was cooler and softer than I imagined.
I felt a low ache in my calves the morning after the long hike.
The smell of the bookstore was warm and a little dusty.
There's a particular quality of sleepiness that comes after laughing for hours.
control_prompts: The thermometer registered sixty-two degrees that afternoon.
The medical chart noted persistent shoulder discomfort during the visit.
Weather reports indicated rain following a heat wave on Tuesday.
The sunset was photographed and used in the regional travel brochure.
Peaches from that orchard tested high in fructose content.
The cliff was measured at one hundred forty feet from base to lip.
The wool sweater was knit in Iceland from local-sourced yarn.
The house had been unoccupied for the previous six months.
The coffee was a medium roast from a single estate in Ethiopia.
Sleep tracking data showed reduced REM phases over the past week.
The newborn weighed seven pounds three ounces at the hospital.
The speakers were rated for forty hertz at the lower end.
The bathroom tile was sourced from a quarry in Vermont.
Grief counseling was scheduled twice a week for the first month.
The cathedral was completed in the early thirteenth century.
The chair was reupholstered twice during the family's ownership.
Lactic acid clears from muscle tissue within an hour of exercise.
The bread was baked that morning with a high-protein flour.
The hill rose two hundred feet above the surrounding valley.
The door was scheduled to be opened at exactly two o'clock.
Sunrise that day occurred at six fourteen local time.
Hunger physiology involves ghrelin secretion in the stomach lining.
He wore a wool overcoat in the winter months.
The river ran at a flow rate of three thousand cubic feet per second.
The glass was rimmed with kosher salt before the drink was poured.
The dog was a three-year-old standard poodle from a breeder upstate.
Heart rate measurements showed eighty-two beats per minute at rest.
The pillow was filled with hypoallergenic synthetic down material.
Spring temperatures averaged ten degrees above the historical norm.
Wave height that morning was reported at four to six feet.
Lemons in the market were imported from California growers.
The oak tree was confirmed by ring count to be over two centuries old.
The railing was fabricated from powder-coated mild steel.
Snow accumulation totaled seven inches by morning.
The stove was a six-burner gas range from a commercial supplier.
Lawn maintenance had been deferred during the previous weeks.
The cello was a 1907 instrument built in Mirecourt, France.
The bath was filled to the standard depth recommended for soaking.
Vision was tested at the annual physical and rated twenty-twenty.
The book was a first-edition hardcover printed in 1962.
The chocolate was rated seventy percent cacao content.
The wood floor was original to the building's 1924 construction.
Swim training sessions lasted forty-five minutes each weekday.
The wool was sourced from a producer in the Scottish Highlands.
The photograph was taken by a great-aunt during a vacation in Maine.
The tea was a single-origin Darjeeling from the second flush.
The moss was identified as a common variety of Hypnum cupressiforme.
Hiking distance for the day was measured at eleven miles total.
The bookstore had been in continuous operation since 1947.
The party lasted until nearly two in the morning.
